exercises.title: Error Rate Calculator
exercises.description: Calculate logical error rates for concatenated codes.
tasks: Implement error rate formula p_L = p_th × (p/p_th)^(2^k)
Given physical rate and threshold, compute rates for levels 0-6
Plot error rate vs concatenation level
Find minimum levels needed for target rate
Compare different base code thresholds
hints: Use logarithms to handle very small numbers
Error rate should decrease only if p < p_th
Doubly exponential means exponent itself is exponential

exercises.title: Resource Estimator
exercises.description: Estimate resources for concatenated code computations.
tasks: Calculate qubits = n^k for various levels
Estimate gates per QEC round at each level
Given circuit size, determine required concatenation
Compute total qubits for M logical qubits
Compare overhead to surface code estimates
hints: Gates per round: g × n^(k-1) at level k

exercises.title: Two-Level Simulator
exercises.description: Simulate error correction in 2-level concatenated code.
tasks: Create 49-qubit state (7 level-1 blocks)
Inject random errors at physical level
Decode each level-1 block
Use corrected blocks for level-2 syndrome
Track correction success rate
hints: Level-2: treat corrected blocks as qubits
Failure: when either level fails to correct

exercises.title: Concatenation Optimizer
exercises.description: Optimize concatenation parameters for target applications.
tasks: Given circuit (size, depth), find optimal levels
Compare different base codes (Steane, [[5,1,3]], Bacon-Shor)
Minimize total physical qubits
Account for time overhead at each level
Output optimal configuration
hints: Different codes have different n and p_th
Time overhead: d rounds × k levels
Balance qubit count vs circuit depth
Consider hybrid: different codes at different levels
